finding: plan_auth_provider/reconcile_auth_provider branches, Option handling, and merge verified correct; no partial write; no panic on fallible paths
all fallible paths (lookup None/Err, update Err) warn/error-log and return; boot never failed by an entry; no unwrap on non-test path
secret never logged (only var name via TemplateError); encrypted at rest via update_provider; inline-literal guard applied to client_secret; shipped yaml/compose/README carry only placeholders
no new permission; runs at boot with no user context; only intended effect is surfacing Google on the public login page
runs under existing advisory lock; UPDATE-by-id of a seeded row (never INSERT) so no duplicate-row race/TOCTOU; boot-time-only; idempotent re-deploy

mirrors McpServerEntry/reconcile_mcp_server idioms (log prefix, warn-on-skip, deny_unknown_fields, Mode, resolve_with/resolve_secret_with, repository encrypt path); ensure-pure-no-op divergence documented and correct for a create-forbidden entity
update_provider arg order/types correct; name:None keeps name via COALESCE; Some(&config) triggers encrypt+client_secret_encrypted write; decrypted-then-overwritten secret leaves no stale value
merge preserves issuer_url/scopes/attribute_mapping/display_name; non-object config -> Skip (no corruption); enabled threaded; stable idempotent outcome
boot-time, single get+single update per entry; no needless scan/round-trips; clone of a tiny config is fine

adding GOOGLE_* to env_for and secrets.storage_key to reboot() does not regress MCP tests (no MCP secret headers, no plaintext-config assertion); rcpa-user->rcpa fixes a genuinely stale test; YAML/compose valid
the ensure-mode early-return branch of reconcile_auth_provider (row left untouched) has no test; a regression that stamped/enabled on mode: ensure would go uncaught
